purpose: Compare fig3 style candidate families before any source, SVG, accepted-state, release-state, export, or golden mutation is authorized.
forbidden_semantic_changes: change panel roles: panel a must remain PDMS clean bandgap and panel b must remain sulfur polymer trap mechanism
rename symbols, CB/VB, E_t, kT, injection, capture, thermal escape, shallow trap, or deep trap labels
swap shallow/deep trap color semantics or invert their relative positions
remove required labels, trapped-electron clusters, trap levels, injection arrows, capture arrows, or weak escape cues
use SVG polish to repair scientific, semantic, or label-target errors that belong in TikZ/source review
benchmark_measurable_checks: style_lock_typography must not add local tiny/scriptsize/huge overrides
visual_clash_delta should decrease_or_equal when rendered artifacts exist
human_only_questions: Does the current restrained two-panel schematic already communicate the mechanism well enough for manuscript use?
Is the remaining gap bounded source polish, or is a broader editorial redesign worth the semantic risk?
Is this figure being promoted toward a flagship visual, or should it remain a restrained main-text concept schematic?
candidate_rejection_rules: reject a prettier candidate if it changes semantic meaning, panel roles, labels, trap positions, or color meaning
reject SVG polish if it is being used to fix source-level semantic defects
reject broad redesign if the only improvement is non-measurable taste language
reject any route that implies accepted-state, release-state, final-artifact, export, or golden mutation without a separate explicit gate

comparison_basis: human decision selected keep_current_style as style policy only
current two-panel mechanism schematic remains the benchmark until a candidate beats it on measurable checks and human art direction
failure_modes: can still be displaced by a later candidate with stronger evidence
does not approve source, SVG, release, accepted-state, export, final-artifact, or golden mutation
prerequisite_evidence: Wave G decision record keeps current style with no_source_mutation boundary
Wave G benchmark pack defines target style class and rejection rules
can_improve: preserve the current generated export as the semantic benchmark for trap-retention mechanism review
reduce release risk by keeping source, SVG, accepted, export, golden, final-artifact, and publication state untouched
forbidden_semantic_changes: must not change panel a/b roles, CB/VB labels, E_t/kT labels, injection/capture/escape arrows, trap positions, or shallow/deep trap color semantics
proof_evidence: fresh render, critique, and export evidence remain non-regressing
no candidate proves better mechanism communication without semantic or release risk
candidate_family_comparisons.human_only_question: Does the restrained two-panel trap schematic already communicate the mechanism well enough for manuscript use?

comparison_basis: candidate may improve typography, trap-level spacing, stroke rhythm, or label breathing room
candidate must improve at least one measurable check while preserving all forbidden semantics
failure_modes: reject if it changes capture, escape, or trap-level meaning while looking cleaner
reject if it only changes taste language without measurable or named aesthetic benefit
prerequisite_evidence: fresh comparison packet after candidate patch exists
can_improve: bounded typography, trap-level spacing, stroke rhythm, label breathing room, or arrow clarity while preserving the mechanism
forbidden_semantic_changes: must not alter capture direction, escape direction, trap placement, trap-energy labels, panel roles, or shallow/deep trap color semantics
proof_evidence: candidate improves a measurable check or named aesthetic lever with benchmark hard-regressions absent
candidate_family_comparisons.human_only_question: Is the remaining gap truly bounded source polish rather than taste-only change?

comparison_basis: candidate could be visually stronger but must beat current style without increasing semantic or release risk
human must explicitly ask for broader visual-language alternatives after seeing benchmark tradeoffs
failure_modes: reject a prettier candidate if it changes semantic meaning
reject if it weakens panel roles, label targets, trap position, color semantics, or mechanism contrast
prerequisite_evidence: human request for editorial redesign benchmark
semantic preservation evidence against all forbidden changes
can_improve: stronger visual language, mechanism contrast, and journal fit if the current two-panel schematic is insufficient
forbidden_semantic_changes: must not weaken panel roles, trap physics, label targets, capture/escape cues, trap positions, or color semantics
proof_evidence: human explicitly requests broader redesign after seeing benchmark tradeoffs
candidate beats current style while preserving all trap-retention semantics
candidate_family_comparisons.human_only_question: Is a broader editorial redesign worth the semantic risk for this mechanism figure?

comparison_basis: SVG polish is only an optical finish route after TikZ/source semantics are locked
semantic or label-target defects must return to TikZ/source review
failure_modes: reject if SVG polish is used to repair scientific, semantic, or label-target defects
reject if no positive ready_for_svg_polish evidence exists
prerequisite_evidence: ready_for_svg_polish evidence required before SVG artifact handoff
separate SVG artifact mutation approval required even after positive evidence
can_improve: optical finish, spacing, stroke polish, and texture nuance after source semantics are locked
forbidden_semantic_changes: must not repair source-level scientific defects, relabel targets, move trap levels, alter arrows, or change shallow/deep color meaning in SVG space
proof_evidence: separate SVG artifact mutation approval exists and TikZ/source semantic checks are already clean
candidate_family_comparisons.human_only_question: Should SVG polish remain blocked until explicit ready_for_svg_polish evidence exists?
